result.markdown:
---
title: "A week without digital notes. The after"
description: "A micro-update nobody asked for."
date: 2026-06-18
categories: [note-taking, PKM, microblogging]
draft: true
---

A few months ago I spent a week without digital notes and measured my notebook like it owed me something. The post is [here](https://cecibaldoni.github.io/blog/week-without-digital.html), if you missed it.

I said at the end that I changed almost nothing. That was true. But "almost" has been sitting in the back of my head.

So here's the data.

## Bullet points, the vengeance


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](week-without-digital-aftermath_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


Bullet point usage went way up. This was not a conscious decision, and I have no tidy explanation for this.

The only thing that I associate this with is the Logseq structure. Not only I started using way more bullet points to resamble the block logic in Logseq, but I even started making *nested* bullet points. 

But then why I did not use any during my digital detox week, and more when Logseq was available? These kind questions keep me up at night.


## But what about pages used?

This data didn't make it into the original post, but someone asked me about this so you're getting it now.



::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](week-without-digital-aftermath_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


Analog usage went up. Not to the heights of the challenge week (that was a forced experiment, with no other outlet), but meaningfully above where it started. 

The notebook is getting more use than before. 

Whether that means the experiment worked or I just used the excuse to buy a new notebook, it will remain a mistery.


## In the end

I ended the [original post](https://cecibaldoni.github.io/blog/week-without-digital.html) saying that I changed almost nothing, because that is how I felt at the time. Immediately after the self-imposed return to technology, my life felt going back to its original axis.

More than 8 years in research should have taught me not to wave those statement without proof: the data says something changedso it must mean that something recalibrated. Which is either the best possible outcome of an experiment or a sign I should run more of them.
